objective: Exercise the joc_work_surface_ui_packet candidate template with a minimal valid work-surface architecture packet.

sections.INTENT: Design a mission cockpit chat work surface that keeps context, proof, and active tool state visible before component implementation starts.

SHELL_ZONES.purpose: Expose current mission, connection state, and bounded action controls.
Provide stable navigation between chat, context, files, proof, and settings lenses.
Hold the active chat or work packet canvas without hiding progress state.
Show selected context, citations, queue packets, and validation receipts.
Display async tool runs, returns, validation status, and settlement events.

sections.USER_PRIMARY_FLOW: A user opens the cockpit, reviews loaded continuity context, asks for work, watches slow tool state, inspects proof, and settles or redirects the next packet.

SCREEN_AND_INTERACTION_STATES.user_feedback: Continuity context and cockpit routes are resolving.
The active tool or carrier run is still pending, with elapsed time visible.
The failed action names the failing surface and gives retry or inspect options.
No packet or source is selected, with the next useful selection available.
A blocked action explains the missing authority without exposing unsafe controls.

COMPONENT_CONTRACTS.testable_obligation: The shell renders stable zones and does not collapse when async state changes.
Context and proof lenses remain distinct and keyboard reachable.

CONTEXT_AND_PROOF_VISUALIZATION: Capsule, mini, source evidence, and command receipts appear as inspectable surfaces rather than hidden backend jargon.
Timeline entries distinguish requested work, running work, returned proof, and validation gaps.
VALIDATION_PLAN: Run focused unit tests for routing and view-model projection.
Run Playwright or screenshot validation before claiming visual completion.
Record any unrun visual proof explicitly in the return contract.
NON_CLAIMS: This is a candidate work-surface planning packet, not accepted UI canon.
No frontend implementation or screenshot proof is claimed by this instance.
No ION/03_registry mutation or product-front-door landing occurred.
sections.SETTLEMENT_ROUTE: JOC_UI_CANON_STEWARD reviews source alignment, then VISUAL_PROOF_AUDITOR checks visual evidence before broad implementation.

proves: required UI work-surface sections can be satisfied
candidate boundary and non-claims can be preserved
slow response, proof, context, and settlement surfaces can be named before implementation

non_claims: This instance is candidate current-context test data, not accepted template law.
This instance does not execute external tools or mutate production state.
